competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume
fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout, RSI 64 not overbought
fill to cap: breakout, RSI 66 not overbought

competitors.name: congress_mirror
competitors.rules: Mirrors the most successful members of Congress: ranks filers by disclosed-trade excess return (kadoa STOCK Act mirror), then buys the names the top 10 disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighted by how many bought each. Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

competitors.name: S&P 500
competitors.rules: Buys SPY with the whole book on the first forward session and holds — the market baseline.

trade_log.reason: buy & hold: all-in on day one

competitors.name: You
competitors.rules: Your real Robinhood account, rebased to the shared origin so it's comparable. Performance only — holdings and trades are deliberately not published.

competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: llm_voters
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

competitors.name: mirofish_real
competitors.rules: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.

analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
analyst.action: Cut the bleeding defensive hedge (UNH), trim the most-extended winners (GE, BAC at RSI 80), rotate into the semis sleeve that leads a de-escalation tape (MU up, add TSM) plus a healthcare-growth diversifier (LLY); hold ~47% cash as the real ballast into the mid-July earnings cluster.
analyst.sizing: 6 names, 53% deployed / 47% cash. Semis MU 0.11 + TSM 0.08; value-financials BAC 0.10 + JPM 0.10; aerospace GE 0.07 (trimmed); healthcare-growth LLY 0.07. Max single name 0.11 (cap 0.60). Cash is the hedge, not a defensive stock.

regime.label: Hawkish Fed, but an energy/inflation-relief risk-on rotation
regime.note: The inflation spike that drove the hawkish Fed was energy-led (Iran war). With US-Iran peace talks set to resume 6/30, that energy/inflation pressure is easing at the margin, sparking a tech/growth/semis risk-on rotation out of defensives. But the Fed is still on hold at 3.50-3.75% with a dot plot that flipped to a HIKE and 17/18 officials seeing upside inflation risk - this is a geopolitical relief rally, not a confirmed dovish pivot. Stay balanced, don't chase high-beta junk, keep dry powder.

analyst.thesis: What changed since 6/26: the regime is inflecting. Last tick I read the hawkish/inflation regime as durable and tilted the book to defensive-cyclical quality (BAC/GE/JPM/MU) plus a UNH defensive hedge. On 6/29 the tape repudiated the defensive half: a +1.6% SPY day (S&P +1.18% to 7,440; Nasdaq +2.07%; Dow above 52,000 first time) led by tech/Mag7/high-beta (RBLX +14%, TSLA +8.6%), with volatility and inverse ETFs collapsing - because the Iran war energy shock that drove the Fed's inflation upgrade is now de-escalating (Trump: US-Iran peace talks resume Tuesday). My defensive ballast bled INTO the rally: UNH -1.85% on an up day. So I cut UNH - a managed-care name at a 52-wk-high PE 32 that isn't acting as ballast and carries 7/16 earnings risk - and bank gains in the two most-extended winners (GE and BAC are both RSI 80, GE at PE 45 and a fresh all-time high). I rotate that capital toward the cleanest expression of an energy-relief / risk-on tape - semis - adding TSM (foundry monopoly, PE 36) alongside a bumped MU (memory/HBM-AI momentum, r20 +18%), and add LLY (secular GLP-1 grower at a fresh 52-wk high, a non-cyclical growth driver that is NOT a dead defensive). I keep the cheap quality financials (BAC, JPM at PE 14-16) into their 7/14 earnings. Crucially I hold ~47% cash: with the rally resting on a fragile 'talks set to resume' headline and a still-hawkish Fed, cash - not a bleeding defensive stock - is my downside hedge, and it's dry powder for the mid-July earnings cluster. This lowers book beta and diversifies drivers (semis / financials / aerospace / healthcare), directly fixing last tick's flaw: one-sided cyclicals that all fell together.

evidence.point: 06-29 was a broad risk-on rally: 'The S&P 500 gained 1.18% and closed at 7,440.43, while the Nasdaq Composite rose 2.07% to 25,820.14. The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed above 52,000 for the first time.' Snapshot SPY 728.99->740.88 (+1.6%).

Rally driver = Iran de-escalation easing the energy/inflation shock: 'easing tensions between the US and Iran over the weekend, with President Trump saying peace talks with Iran are set to resume on Tuesday.'

Fed remains hawkish: held at 3.50-3.75%, 'The grid erased an earlier indication for one cut this year and pushed any reductions into 2027 and 2028'; PCE raised to 3.6% from 2.7%, core to 3.3%; '17 of 18 officials judged the risks to inflation to be tilted to the upside.'
UNH bled into the rally (defensive thesis broken): UNH -1.85% on a +1.6% tape; PE 32.32; 52-wk high 427.93 set 6/26 - i.e. selling off straight from the high. Q2 report 2026-07-16.
GE and BAC are the most-extended winners to trim: both RSI ~80; GE PE 45.48 at a fresh 52-wk high (379.67 on 6/25); BAC PE 14.36 also at a 52-wk high (59.195 on 6/25).
Semis lead the rotation: MU PE 25.63, 20-day return +17.9%, RSI 60 (room to run, not overbought); TSM PE 35.93, 52-wk position 92%, foundry to the whole AI complex.
LLY printed a fresh 52-wk high today: high_52_weeks 1238.00 dated 2026-06-29, 5-day return +11.5% - secular GLP-1 growth, a non-cyclical driver distinct from semis/banks.
Mid-July earnings cluster is the next catalyst: BAC & JPM report 2026-07-14 (est EPS 1.10 / 5.48), ASML 7/15, GE & TSM 7/16 (est 1.85 / 3.77). ~11-13 ticks out.

risks: The rally rests on a fragile headline - peace talks are only 'set to resume,' not concluded. If Iran talks collapse, energy re-spikes, the inflation/hawkish trade snaps back and risk-on reverses; my 47% cash is the hedge but the semis adds (MU/TSM) would lead the drop.

Two semis names (MU + TSM = 18%) can fall together - the same 'high-beta cyclicals correlate' failure that hurt last tick; sized modestly and split memory vs foundry to mitigate, but it remains a concentrated factor.

Fed is still hawkish (dot plot flipped to a hike, 17/18 see upside inflation risk). A hot inflation print or hawkish Fedspeak would pressure the rich, high-multiple names (GE PE 45, TSM PE 36, LLY PE 43).
Cutting UNH right before its 7/16 earnings forfeits any beat; accepted because it's a defensive that isn't defending and sits at a 52-wk-high valuation.
Buys fill at the 6/30 open after a sharp up day - gap-up risk means I may chase MU/TSM/LLY a bit higher than the 6/29 close (orders are market-on-open).

data_examined.label: 06-29 daily bars for all 101 names (built from RH minute aggregation; day feed still lagged at 06-26)
Quant screen over the shuffled universe: 1/5/20-day returns, 52-wk position, RSI(14), 20/50-SMA, volume ratio
Fundamentals (PE, P/B, div, 52-wk range, market cap) for MU/BAC/GE/JPM/UNH/ASML/TSM/LLY/AMD/DE
Macro: 06-29 rally drivers + June FOMC outcome/dot plot

rationale.MU: Bumped 0.08->0.11, the book's top conviction. [DATA] Memory/HBM-AI momentum leader: 20-day return +17.9%, RSI 60 (room before overbought), PE 25.63 - the cheapest of the AI-semis on earnings, already past its 6/18 earnings gap. [ASSUMED] Semis are the cleanest expression of an energy-relief / risk-on rotation, and MU's memory cycle is a distinct sub-driver from foundry. Risk: high-beta, reversed hard once already (the 6/26 -7.5% intraday).
rationale.BAC: Trimmed 0.13->0.10. [DATA] The book's value anchor and cheapest name: PE 14.36, P/B 1.50, 1.9% yield, Q2 earnings 2026-07-14 (est EPS 1.10). [ASSUMED] Trimming because it's now RSI ~80 at a 52-wk high (59.195) - bank some of the run, keep the core into earnings. Lowest-beta way to stay long quality.
rationale.JPM: Held ~0.08->0.10. [DATA] Quality financial, PE 15.76, P/B 2.56, 1.8% yield, RSI 65 - the least-extended of my financials (52-wk pos 89% vs BAC/GE at the high). Q2 earnings 2026-07-14 (est EPS 5.48). [ASSUMED] Best-in-class bank; keep full size into the 7/14 catalyst since it has more room than BAC.
rationale.TSM: New initiation 0.08. [DATA] Foundry monopoly to the entire AI/semis complex, PE 35.93 - cheaper than ASML (PE 60) or AMD (PE 171); 52-wk position 92%; Q2 earnings 2026-07-16 (est EPS 3.77). [ASSUMED] Adds semis breadth (foundry vs MU's memory) to ride the de-escalation/risk-on rotation without chasing the high-beta junk (RBLX/TSLA/MSTR) that ripped today.
rationale.GE: Trimmed hard 0.13->0.07. [DATA] Aerospace quality, but the most-extended/richest name held: RSI ~80, PE 45.48, at a fresh 52-wk high (379.67). Q2 earnings 2026-07-16 (est EPS 1.85). [ASSUMED] Bank most of the gain - it's run too far too fast - but keep a core because the commercial-aero services franchise is structurally strong and reports into the cluster.
rationale.LLY: New initiation 0.07. [DATA] Secular GLP-1 grower printing a fresh 52-wk high today (1238.00, dated 6/29), 5-day return +11.5%, PE 43.49. [ASSUMED] A non-cyclical growth diversifier with its own momentum - distinct from semis and banks, and the right kind of healthcare to own here (growth, not the bleeding managed-care defensive UNH I'm cutting). Earnings are early-Aug, so no near-term event risk.

reflection.looking_back: Last tick I de-risked the cyclical book: cut CAT, trimmed MU/JPM/GE, and initiated a UNH defensive hedge, holding BAC 0.13 / GE 0.13 / UNH 0.12 / MU 0.08 / JPM 0.08 with ~46% cash. The book gave up alpha on 6/29: it finished roughly flat-to-up small on the day while SPY ran +1.6%. All-time the book is -10.3% and -7.6 pts behind SPY; last tick -1.8% vs SPY -0.6% (alpha -1.3 pts).
worked: The de-risking instinct was directionally right - cutting CAT and trimming MU banked gains before further chop; BAC and GE as quality anchors are both at 52-wk highs (RSI 80) and carried the book.
Keeping a MU core paid: +17.9% over 20 days and +2.0% on 6/29, validating not panic-selling on the 6/26 -7.5% scare.
missed: The UNH defensive hedge was the wrong instrument: it fell -1.85% on a +1.6% up day - a defensive that doesn't defend - because I mis-read the regime as durably hawkish right as the Iran energy shock began to de-escalate.

The book was positioned defensive into a risk-on inflection: ~46% cash plus a defensive/value tilt meant I barely participated in a +1.6% SPY day led by tech/semis - the alpha leak was being cautious in the wrong direction.
reflection.adjustment: Re-read the regime off the energy/inflation driver, not the stale 'hawkish forever' frame: rotate the defensive sleeve (cut UNH) into the semis that lead a de-escalation tape (add TSM, bump MU) and a healthcare-growth name (LLY), and make CASH - not a bleeding defensive stock - the explicit downside hedge.

swarm.note: Live swarm — 149 fish, each a unique trader profile shown its own random ~20-name slice of the watchlist (with recent headlines), voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Connected to the Agentic cash account •••• — currently unfunded ($0). Deposit into this specific account to trade the real $100. No agent orders placed yet.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.llm_voters: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.
rules.mirofish_real: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.
rules.congress_mirror: Mirrors the most successful members of Congress: ranks filers by disclosed-trade excess return (kadoa STOCK Act mirror), then buys the names the top 10 disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighted by how many bought each. Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2026-01-02–2026-06-29, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Real-MiroFish swarm
sources.detail: Social-simulation swarm (bot/mirofish.py): persona agents with memory interact over multiple rounds — each re-ranks its best ideas seeing its own prior view and its neighbours' latest — so a consensus forms via peer influence (the opposite of the independent swarm). The panel's rank-weighted (Borda) consensus becomes a multi-name book (top 5 names, weight ∝ points). Tiered cost (cheap/default/qwen); seeded from the briefing + optional world-events news.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.

sources.name: Display scale
sources.detail: The real paper books are $100 each; every dollar and share figure on this site is shown ×100 (a $10,000 book) so holdings read in whole shares and dollars. Per-share prices and percentages are unscaled.
